text.title: QLoRA — 4-bit Quantization with LoRA Adapters
text.summary: BitsAndBytesConfig for 4-bit NF4 quantization, prepare_model_for_kbit_training ordering requirement, and memory savings table by model size.

rule.statement: Call prepare_model_for_kbit_training() BEFORE get_peft_model() — reversing the order causes silent training failure.
rule.rationale: prepare_model_for_kbit_training() configures gradient checkpointing and layer normalization for 4-bit training; applying LoRA before this produces incompatible layer states.

example.code:
from transformers import BitsAndBytesConfig, AutoModelForCausalLM from peft import prepare_model_for_kbit_training, get_peft_model import torch
bnb_config = BitsAndBytesConfig( load_in_4bit=True, bnb_4bit_quant_type="nf4", bnb_4bit_compute_dtype=torch.bfloat16, bnb_4bit_use_double_quant=True, ) model = AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained( "meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B", quantization_config=bnb_config, device_map="auto", ) model = prepare_model_for_kbit_training(model) # MUST come before get_peft_model model = get_peft_model(model, lora_config)
example.note: bnb_4bit_compute_dtype=bfloat16 avoids NaN loss on A100/H100. Use float16 on older GPUs.

list.item: 7B model: full FT 28GB → LoRA fp16 16GB → QLoRA 4-bit 6-8GB
13B model: full FT 52GB → LoRA fp16 28GB → QLoRA 4-bit 10-12GB
70B model: full FT 280GB → LoRA fp16 140GB → QLoRA 4-bit 40-48GB

rule.statement: Use paged_adamw_8bit optimizer only when bitsandbytes is installed and CUDA is available — fall back to adamw_torch for CPU training.
rule.rationale: paged_adamw_8bit is a bitsandbytes-specific optimizer that uses paged memory to handle optimizer state spikes; it is not available in non-CUDA environments.
